rulings.Case Title: DOUGLAS D COLES ET AL VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Defendant Regents Of The University Of California For Summary Judgment Against Plaintiff Erlinda T. Ritz Only
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY, AUGUST 9, 2018, LINE 2 DEFENDANT REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Against Plaintiff Erlinda T. Ritz Only Judge Kahn discloses that the General Counsel of the University of California is a close friend of his, but neither that friendship nor any other matter causes him to be other than fair and impartial in deciding this case. Defendant The Regents of the University of California's motion for summary judgment against plaintiff Erlinda Ritz only is granted. No opposition filed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling adversely affects, in whole or in part, any party who is not represented by an attorney, then any other party (whether represented by an attorney or not) may submit on the tentative ruling without appearing for the hearing only if the adversely affected self-represented party has stated in writing or orally that it, he or she will not appear at the hearing. Counsel for the Regents is required to prepare a proposed order which complies with CCP 437c(g) and states that the motion was not opposed and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. = HEK

rulings.Case Title: MINT COLLECTION -410-418 JESSIE STREET CONDOMINIUM VS. FIFTH HISTORIC PROPERTIES, LLC, ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Mint Collection, 401-418 Jessie Street Condominium Owners' Association'S Motion To Compel Martin Mcnerney Development, Inc., 418 Jessie Historic Properties, Llc And 418 Jessie Propoerties, Llc To Answer Interrogatory
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, August 9, 2018, Line 2, Plaintiff Mint Collection, 401-418 Jessie Street Condominium Owners' Association'S Motion To Compel Martin Mcnerney Development, Inc., 418 Jessie Historic Properties, Llc And 418 Jessie Propoerties, Llc To Answer Interrogatory. Pro Tem Judge Sanford Kingsley, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing Required. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to skmediate@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: CHRISTINA FERNANDEZ VS. SEPHORA USA, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Sephora Usa Inc.'S Motion For Order Compelling Complaince With Business Records Subpoena (Added Per Ex Parte Order Filed 8/1/18)
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY, AUGUST 9, 2018, LINE 4, Motion 2 Defendant Sephora Usa Inc.'S Motion For Order Compelling Complaince With Business Records Subpoena to nonparty Kaiser Permanente GRANTED in part unless prior to the hearing counsel for plaintiff Christina Fernandez provides a CCP 2032.320(c) stipulation to counsel for Sephora and includes in that stipulation additional language that neither Ms. Fernandez nor any other witness called by Ms. Fernandez will testify at trial or make any argument about any medical services Ms. Fernandez received for any mental health condition or emotional distress. If the stipulation is provided, the motion is DENIED. If the stipulation is not provided, no later than August 16, 2018 Kaiser is required to comply with the subpoena except that Kaiser should not produce any documents for any time period August 14, 2015 or earlier per the parties' prior agreement. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Sephora is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. = HEK

rulings.Case Title: CHRISTINA FERNANDEZ VS. SEPHORA USA, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Sephora Usa, Inc.'S Motion To Compel (1)Responses To Special Interrogatories, Set One; And (2) Responses To Document Requests, Set Two; And For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY, AUGUST 9, 2018, LINE 4, Motion 3 Sephora Usa, Inc.'S Motion To Compel (1)Responses To Special Interrogatories, Set One; And (2) Responses To Document Requests, Set Two; And (3) For Monetary Sanctions GRANTED in part. While the issue is close, the court concludes that CCP 2016.060 extends the discovery cutoff date from a Saturday to a Monday where the trial date is on a Monday. Much of the discovery propounded by Sephora is fairly characterized as contention discovery which has limited but not no utility when served at the end of the discovery period. Most of the discovery requests are sufficiently narrowly tailored to seek only discovery relevant information whose likely probative value outweighs the burden on Ms. Fernandez to provide the information at this late stage of the case. Ms. Fernandez is not required to provide further response to the discovery requests that are not so narrowly tailored or seek information whose likely probative value is outweighed by the burden on Ms. Fernandez to provide the information at this late stage of the case. No later than August 20, 2018 plaintiff Christina Fernandez is required to provide verified code-compliant further responses to special interrogatories 1-3, 5-10, 20 and 23 and document request 6 and produce all responsive documents in her possession, custody and control. Unless Ms. Fernandez states in writing that neither she nor any witness called by her will testify about her suffering from postpartum depression while she was on leave from Sephora or after the termination of her employment by Sephora, Ms. Fernandez is also required to provide verified code-compliant further responses to special interrogatories 13-19 and 22 and document requests 1-2 and produce all responsive documents in her possession, custody and control. Ms. Fernandez is not required to provide any further responses to special interrogatories 4, 11-12, 21, and 24 and document requests 3-5. Because Ms. Fernandez's position regarding the timeliness of the discovery requests did not lack substantial justification, Sephora's request for monetary sanctions is denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Sephora is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. = HEK

rulings.Case Title: DAVID MOURRA ET AL VS. ROMAN SHTYLMAN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION - Notice Of Hearing And Motion For Relief From Order Pursuant To Ccp 473
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY, AUGUST 9, 2018, LINE 6 DEFENDANTS ROMAN SHTYLMAN and BITFLOOR, INC.'s MOTION For Relief From Order Pursuant To C.C.P. Section 473 DENIED. Back in June, I overruled defendants' demurrer asserting lack of personal jurisdiction, because it was contrary to California law. "Lack of personal jurisdiction over defendant is not ground for demurrer. It must be raised, if at all, by motion to quash service of summons [CCP §418.10¿] If defendant instead demurs, the demurrer constitutes a general appearance, waiving any defect in personal jurisdiction [CCP §1014]". (Weil & Brown, California Practice Guide: Civil Procedure Before Trial (TRG 2016) p. 7(I)-36 (emphasis in original); see also id at p. 3-106.) Defendants now move for relief under Code of Civil Procedure §473, conceding their "mistake," but claiming it was "excusable." However, defendants also concede that whether a mistake is excusable is judged by whether a "reasonably prudent" attorney would have made it. (See Zamora v. Clayborn Contracting Group, Inc. (2002) 28 Cal.4th 249, 258.) Reasonably prudent California litigation lawyers know the requirements for challenging personal jurisdiction. Even attorneys who do not know the rules can ascertain them in a matter of seconds - e.g., by consulting the Weil & Brown treatise. Moreover, defendants cite no authority holding that, once waived, a lack of personal jurisdiction claim can be revived. Defendants say their New York lawyers drafted the demurrers, but pro hac vice counsel are subject to the same professional standards as California counsel. (See Weil & Brown, p. 9(I)-168; CRC 9.40.) Defendants' motion is DENIED. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. = RBU
